show "Test Passed" when a tests pass, not what was evaluated","user":{"login":"nickrobinson251","id":13448787,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEzNDQ4Nzg3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/13448787?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/nickrobinson251","html_url":"https://github.com/nickrobinson251","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nickrobinson251/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nickrobinson251/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nickrobinson251/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nickrobinson251/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nickrobinson251/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nickrobinson251/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nickrobinson251/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nickrobinson251/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/nickrobinson251/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2022-01-13T15:20:02Z","updated_at":"2022-01-13T15:20:02Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"This is a follow-up to https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/36879, to request we reconsider the effect this change had on how tests which pass are displayed.

That PR changed `Tests.Pass` objects to store extra info about the expression and data passed to `@test`, but storing this info also led to a change in what was displayed when a test passes. Before only "Test Passed" was displayed, and after the "Expression/Evaluated" was also printed e.g.

In Julia v1.6:
```jl
julia> @test 1 == 1
Test Passed
```
In Julia v1.7:
```jl
julia> @test 1 == 1
Test Passed
  Expression: 1 == 1
   Evaluated: 1 == 1
```

The PR was intending to address issue #25483 which only requested the info be stored, i believe so that _custom_ Testsets could make use of this info (e.g. display), not also suggesting a change to what's displayed by `Tests.@test`. So it seems that the change to the printed output was not particularly requested by anyone. And I think the change is a little unfortunate for a couple reasons:
1. It is a significant interface change for those who run lots of tests in the REPL. Previously, verbose output (showing Expression/Evaluated) indicated that the test had failed. Seeing this output has come to indicate "something's wrong".
2. The printing of the Expression/Evaluated info can easily push the most crucial information off the screen i.e. did the test pass or not? This was not so much of an issue before when verbose output itself indicated the test did not pass. 

As an example, here's an example of how a passing tests comparing two DataFrames looks the REPL:
```jl
julia> X = rand(300, 300);

julia> df = DataFrame(X, :auto);

julia> @test df == df
```
![Screenshot 2022-01-13 at 15 09 08](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13448787/149355844-6beafe19-80e1-4786-8aee-ffa7f2194411.png)

whereas in v1.6:
![Screenshot 2022-01-13 at 15 11 29](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/13448787/149356228-db0c7f1f-3c29-4b59-b7f2-a32f6f001b0d.png)

---

@mmiller-max points out (on Slack) that the printing change resulting from the PR were only briefly discussed (see [this discussion](https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/36879#discussion_r468892636)). And that the conclusion of that changes to the printing code (which were originally in the PR to keep printing _behaviour_ closer to what it was before the change) weren't really applicable to the PR and should be removed from the PR. So leaves it open for a further discussion I think what the actual printing _behaviour_ should be. 

Personally I think the behaviour should be that tests which pass display only "Test Passed". 

@mmiller-max says one reason for not implementing printing code changes in that PR was that it was actually inconsistent as to what a passing test displayed on 1.6, as `@test_throws` still showed some additional output, unlike standard `@test`:
```jl
julia> @test_throws ArgumentError throw(ArgumentError("a"))
Test Passed
      Thrown: ArgumentError
```
So i suggest we make both consistent in only showing "Test Passed", since the verbose output actually masks the crucial info of whether or not the test passed.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/43794/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/43794/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null}}
